subject: Add CSIT verify jobs to DMaaP
commit_message:
Add CSIT verify jobs to DMaaP

Issue-ID: DMAAP-1551
Signed-off-by: Lasse Kaihlavirta <l.kaihlavirt@partner.samsung.com>
Change-Id: I63d94fb563f5b0ef2570201a730e9736700811d8

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ci-management-jjb-verify/5343/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ci-management-jjb-verify/5343

Patch Set 1:

Hi Lasse.
I don't really see the benefit of the verify jobs.
Are they really necessary?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Hi Lasse.
> I don't really see the benefit of the verify jobs.
> Are they really necessary?

Yes. Verify jobs are the only way to verify changes to CSIT test themselves in integration/csit repo before merging them (I started wondering why I'm getting no +1 verify from Jenkins in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/csit/+/118102 and found out that the jobs don't exist at all)

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Hi Lasse.
> > I don't really see the benefit of the verify jobs.
> > Are they really necessary?
> 
> Yes. Verify jobs are the only way to verify changes to CSIT test themselves in integration/csit repo before merging them (I started wondering why I'm getting no +1 verify from Jenkins in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/csit/+/118102 and found out that the jobs don't exist at all)

Ok. I removed them from all DMaaP projects as I didn't see the logic in having them.
Assuming that if someone is making changes to the csit's, they would verify them locally before pushing anything.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > Hi Lasse.
> > > I don't really see the benefit of the verify jobs.
> > > Are they really necessary?
> > 
> > Yes. Verify jobs are the only way to verify changes to CSIT test themselves in integration/csit repo before merging them (I started wondering why I'm getting no +1 verify from Jenkins in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/csit/+/118102 and found out that the jobs don't exist at all)
> 
> Ok. I removed them from all DMaaP projects as I didn't see the logic in having them.
> Assuming that if someone is making changes to the csit's, they would verify them locally before pushing anything.

Local verification before commit is certainly a best practice, but CSIT changes still need to be verified in Jenkins as well to
- make sure that the changes work also in Jenkins (i.e. everything necessary was committed, there are no Jenkins env specific surprises, etc)
- have easily shared public reference for results (be they failing or successful)
- have the required +1 verify automatically (otherwise someone in integration team needs to add it manually every time, and typically we don't know if the changes actually work)

Of course, much of this complexity evaporates if you move your CSITs to DMaaP repository :) https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Project-specific+CSIT+structure
Change has been successfully merged by Lasse Kaihlavirta
